detaileddescription.para: Default distortion vector size.
None.

briefdescription.para: Long range stream. Under normal operations long range stream has a maximum frequency of 5fps and a range from 1m up to 5m, in some cases this can go as far 7.5m.
Short range stream. Under normal operations short range stream has a maximum frequency of 60fps and a range from 0.2m up to 0.9m.

detaileddescription.para: Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration of depth camera streams.
Frame captured using MLDepthCameraStream_LongRange stream.
Frame captured using MLDepthCameraStream_ShortRange stream.

detaileddescription.para: Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration of camera stream used when capturing a frame.

detaileddescription.para: Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration of possible frame rates.

detaileddescription.para: None.
Enable MLDepthCameraDepthImage. See MLDepthCameraDepthImage for more details.
Enable MLDepthCameraConfidenceBuffer. See MLDepthCameraConfidenceBuffer for more details.
Enable MLDepthCameraDepthFlagsBuffer. See MLDepthCameraDepthFlagsBuffer for more details.
Enable MLDepthCameraAmbientRawDepthImage. See MLDepthCameraAmbientRawDepthImage for more details.
Enable MLDepthCameraRawDepthImage. See MLDepthCameraRawDepthImage for more details.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration of flags to select data requested from depth camera.
Valid pixel. Indicates that there is no additional flag data for this pixel.

Invalid. This bit is set to one to indicate that one or more flags from below have been set. Depending on the use case the application can correlate the flag data and corresponding pixel data to determine how to handle the pixel data.
Pixel saturated. The pixel intensity is either below the min or the max threshold value.
Inconsistent data. Inconsistent data received when capturing frames. This can happen due to fast motion.
Low signal. Pixel has very low signal to noise ratio. One example of when this can happen is for pixels in far end of the range.

Flying pixel. This typically happens when there is step jump in the distance of adjoining pixels in the scene. Example: When you open a door looking into the room the edges along the door's edges can cause flying pixels.
Masked. If this bit is on it indicates that the corresponding pixel may not be within the illuminator's illumination cone.
SBI. This bit will be set when there is high noise.
Stray light. This could happen when there is another light source apart from the depth camera illuminator. This could also lead to MLDepthCameraDepthFlags_LowSignal.
Connected component. If a small group of MLDepthCameraDepthFlags_Valid is surrounded by a set of MLDepthCameraDepthFlags_Invalid then this bit will be set to 1.

detaileddescription.para: Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration of flags to select data requested from depth camera.
Enumeration of depth camera streams.
Enumeration of camera stream used when capturing a frame.
Enumeration of possible frame rates.
Enumeration of flags to select data requested from depth camera.
Enumeration of flags to select data requested from depth camera.
Structure to encapsulate the camera config for a specific stream.
Structure to encapsulate the camera settings.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLDepthCameraSettingsInit before use.

These are supported configurations: Streams Exposure limits [us] Frame rate [FPS] Long Range 250 - 2000 1, 5 Short Range 50 - 375 On 50Hz PLC: 5, 25, 50On 60Hz PLC: 5, 30, 60 Use MLDepthCameraGetCapabilities to get a list of available configurations. 
ote The system may not be able to service all the requested streams at any given time. This parameter is treated as a hint and data will be provided for the requested streams if available. 
ote The API only supports a single stream. In a future release support for multiple streams will be added. @par API Level:
 29

briefdescription.para: Depth camera intrinsic parameters.
Structure to encapsulate per plane info for each camera frame.
Depth image.

detaileddescription.para: Depth image stores the depth data from the depth camera. Depth is represented in meters and gives the radial distance of the real world location from the depth camera coordinate frame. See MLDepthCameraIntrinsics and MLDepthCameraData.camera_pose for more details.
Cast the MLDepthCameraFrameBuffer.data to float data type.

briefdescription.para: Confidence score.

detaileddescription.para: Confidence buffer stores the confidence values for each pixel in the MLDepthCameraDepthImage. The confidence score is derived from the sensor noise and it is not normalized. The higher the value the higher the confidence. Applications can determine what confidence threshold to use based on their use case.
Cast the MLDepthCameraFrameBuffer.data to float data type.

briefdescription.para: Flags.

detaileddescription.para: Each pixel in the MLDepthCameraDepthImage will have an associated set of flags.
Cast the MLDepthCameraFrameBuffer.data to uint32_t data type.

briefdescription.para: Ambient raw depth image.

detaileddescription.para: This is the raw depth camera sensor data captured without the depth camera illumination and corresponds to the amount of ambient light incident on the sensor.
Cast the MLDepthCameraFrameBuffer.data to float data type.

briefdescription.para: Raw depth image.

detaileddescription.para: This is the raw depth camera sensor data captured with the depth camera illumination and corresponds to the amount of total light incident on the sensor.
Cast the MLDepthCameraFrameBuffer.data to float data type.

briefdescription.para: Structure to encapsulate output data for each camera sensor.
Structure to encapsulate output data for each camera stream.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLDepthCameraDataInit before use.

briefdescription.para: Structure to encapsulate a possible configuration for a single stream. Can be used to understand possible values for a specific #stream_configs element in MLDepthCameraSettings.

detaileddescription.para: The capabilities supported by the depth camera can be queried with MLDepthCameraGetCapabilities().

briefdescription.para: Structure to encapsulate a possible set of streams configuration. Such set describes a possible way of setting #stream_configs in MLDepthCameraSettings and may hold multiple MLDepthCameraStreamCapability for different streams.

detaileddescription.para: Whole #stream_capabilities array describe a single configuration. Using only part of it is not recommended.
The capabilities supported by the depth camera can be queried with MLDepthCameraGetCapabilities().

briefdescription.para: Structure to encapsulate a list of possible stream configurations.

detaileddescription.para: User should be able to use any of the capabilities from the list. They are grouped in usable sets of configurations - one set may contain different MLDepthCameraStreamCapability for different streams. For more information see MLDepthCameraCapability.

briefdescription.para: Structure to encapsulate camera capabilities filtering.

detaileddescription.para: The capabilities supported by the depth camera can be queried with MLDepthCameraGetCapabilities().

briefdescription.para: Initialize the connect handle structure. Shall be called before calling MLDepthCameraConnect().
Initialize MLDepthCameraData with version.

parameterdescription.para: Set up the version for inout_depth_camera_data.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the capabilities filtering structure. Shall be called before calling MLDepthCameraGetCapabilities().
Connect to depth camera.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to camera handle to be used in later APIs.
One of the parameters is invalid.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

briefdescription.para: Update the depth camera settings.
Query the depth camera stream capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: Retrieves the capabilities supported by the depth camera based on the provided filter. Overview of the capabilities can be seen in the configuration table.
After each call, a corresponding call to MLDepthCameraReleaseCapabilities should be made to release allocated resources.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to initialized MLDepthCameraCapabilityFilter structure. Used for filtering.
One of the parameters is invalid.

briefdescription.para: Release resources allocated with MLDepthCameraGetCapabilities.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to MLDepthCameraCapabilityList filled by the call to MLDepthCameraGetCapabilities.
One of the parameters is invalid.

briefdescription.para: Poll for Frames.

detaileddescription.para: Returns MLDepthCameraData with this latest data when available. The memory is owned by the system. Application should copy the data it needs to cache it and then release the memory by calling #MLDepthCameraReleaseCameraData.
This is a blocking call. API is not thread safe.
If there are no new depth data frames within the timeout_ms duration then the API will return MLResult_Timeout.

parameterdescription.para: Depth camera data. Should be an initialized MLDepthCameraData object.
Returned because no new frame available at this time.

briefdescription.para: Releases specified MLDepthCameraData object.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called exactly once for each successfull call to #MLDepthCameraGetLatestCameraData.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a valid MLDepthCameraData object.

briefdescription.para: Disconnect from depth camera.

detaileddescription.para: This will disconnect from the depth camera.
